scope.request: audio brief script from fixture Feed Items

time_range.label: Fixture Feed Items for audio brief script eval

scope.selection_rule: Grouped the fixture Feed Items into exactly two Artifact Topic units: one for show-style story setup and two-host structure, and one for post-review segment manifest readiness.

units.title: A useful audio brief starts with story setup and a real second host
units.claim: The script should open like a show, explain what happened and why it matters, then use the second host to ask clarifying questions instead of repeating the lead.

supporting_evidence.title: Audio scripts need story setup before analysis
supporting_evidence.reason: The item states that strong sections open with concrete events, listener relevance, and host turns that clarify the story.

supporting_evidence.title: Second hosts should ask useful clarifying questions
supporting_evidence.reason: The item says the second host should act as a listener proxy and translate dense claims into spoken meaning.

units.selection_rationale: This is the lead unit because it defines the editorial behavior the Show Script must demonstrate before any audio rendering is considered.

units.title: Reviewed scripts become resumable segment manifests
units.claim: After Script Review is ready, the local helper can convert the Show Script into a segment manifest that preserves speaker, text, voice metadata, and evidence links for later rendering.

supporting_evidence.title: Segment manifests make audio rendering resumable
supporting_evidence.reason: The item explains that segment manifests are generated from reviewed Show Scripts and make provider rendering resumable.

units.selection_rationale: This unit is included because the requested path stops at the script-only and segment-manifest boundary, before provider rendering or assembly.
